title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
text_en: You or someone you care about may have been diagnosed with Anemia as a result of chronic kidney disease or CKD. This video will help you understand some of the available treatment options for CKD

title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
text_en: Anemia means your blood does not have enough healthy red blood cells to deliver oxygen to the body.

title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
text_en: Anemia can result from chronic kidney disease. Normally, the kidneys make a hormone called erythropoyetin. This hormone helps bone marrow make new red blood cells. When the kidneys are damaged, they don't make enough of this hormone. As a result, bone marrow doesn't make enough red blood cells causing CKD Anemia. This means that organs and tissues may not work as well as they should.

title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
text_en: Anemia treatment options for CKD Anemia may include one or more of the following. An erythropoyetin stimulating agent, iron, and the blood transfusion.

title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
text_en: An erythropoyetin stimulating agent or ESA helps your bone marrow make new red blood cells.

title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
text_en: Another treatment option is iron. For Dr. May prescribed oral iron, this means pills taken by mouth. Or you may receive intravenous iron therapy. In this procedure, iron is delivered through an IV to increase the amount of iron in your

title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
text_en: For severe anemia, you may need a blood transfusion. This means you will be given red blood cells and iron through an IV. It will quickly increase the amount of these substances in your blood.

title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
text_en: If you have questions about treatments for CKD Anemia or any medications you have been prescribed, talk to your doctor. It is important to take your medications as directed and report any side effects you have.
